Uenno...she... Okay, Zojja, let's back up.  Uenno and I decided to stay in the citadel for a few nights after we cleaned shop in the southern Shiverpeaks. Rytlock is with the others in Amnoon, apparently, so no chance of crossing paths. Uenno told me that she needed to meet with another "friend," which was starting to feel a bit suspect every other village... So I followed her. Maaaybe an invasion of privacy, but... I don't know how to feel about what happened yet.  They met just outside of Smokestead. He looked human from a distance, but I suddenly felt  a pinch behind my left ear and sick to my stomach. Whatever spell he was using to conceal his identity was strong. The briefest flash of light, and then...a mursaat. A living mursaat. I...I'd heard about what happened with Lazarus from a Pact engineer in Lion's Arch, but...here...? I was ready to attack when he noticed my position. I tried to run, but my legs went numb.   Uenno urged me to remain calm and went straight into a lecture on personal security (I don't know if it was for me or her). The mursaat hovered nearby and watched. Was he anxious...? He gently put a hand on Uenno's shoulder and pushed her aside. He knelt by me and mumbled something I couldn't make out—it didn't sound like any native tongue I'm familiar with. I could feel my legs again, though. My weapon was just out of reach, but his voice was...kind. He gave me his name and assured my safety. He already knew who I was, and he seemed honored to meet me.
Uh, still...we should vacate this hot hole ASAP.
Ulgoth the Mighty is challenging the Seraph for control over the northern Harathi Hinterlands.

Ulrich and Judge are staring at me right now. It's like they can't look away. I can tell they're disappointed in me. "Poor Tatyanna, with her untamed hair and chewed nails and her whispering and her breathing and hunger. Look at her ribs and her eyes and the dirt on her clothes. Look at her wither and rot." Ha! Their eyes are filled with thoughts and resentment, but I do not care. It's fine, because I found six rocks and lined them all up in a row. Six days until he saves us...until he rewards MY devotion. I think I will eat one each day to show him that I am ready—to ground my body to this place. I don't need food or Ulrich or Judge to keep me alive.    I heard Ulrich call me "delicate" behind my back—they forget that I have ears and that I listen. But it is they who are ignorant. It is not Saul or Caudecus who guides us here. They were simply conduits to a greater plan. And I am now his vassal. I will be welcomed into his arms first and all will be well. It'll be warm and there will be enough food to live on for the rest of my life. I will see my sister and brother, and Ulrich and Judge will stop staring at me. I will be the last happy one.

Unknown to most, the duchess was in possession of a relic stolen from Orr early in the Third Guild War. The Veil of Ilya, an elegant but otherwise visibly unmarkable domino mask, was rumored to pierce the curtain of reality, to look upon things not normally seen by mortal eyes. Its passive magic would allow the wearer to perceive things hidden by illusion or that exist out of phase with Tyria proper as if they were there normally. Should the wearer actively draw upon the Veil's well of power, they may reveal these things to those around them within a defined space and for a short period of time.
